title: Recovery of malonic acid and its esters
abstract: Methods and materials related to producing malonic acid and its esters are disclosed. Specifically, isolated nucleic acids, polypeptides, host cells, and methods and materials for producing malonic acid and its esters by direct fermentation from sugars are disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of producing a malonate ester, comprising: fermenting a recombinant host cell to produce malonic acid; contacting the malonic acid with an alkali metal base or ammonium base to produce a malonic acid salt; isolating the malonic acid salt; esterifying the isolated malonic acid salt with an alcohol using supercritical carbon dioxide (sCC ) or sulfuric acid as a catalyst; and distilling the malonate ester. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the recombinant host cell comprises one or more heterologous nucleic acids encoding a malonyl-CoA hydrolase. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the recombinant host cell comprises one or more heterologous nucleic acids encoding a mutated 3-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA hydrolase derived from Pseudomonas fulva. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the mutations are selected from E95N, Q348A, F304R and combinations thereof. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein the fermentation is performed in a broth comprising at least one carbon source and one nitrogen source. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the carbon source is selected from monosaccharides, disaccharides, polysaccharides, glycerol, acetate, ethanol, methanol, methane and combinations thereof. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the fermentation is performed under aerobic conditions. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the malonic acid is contacted with an alkali metal base selected from sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and combinations thereof. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the malonic acid salt is selected from Na 2 M, K 2 M and Na 2 M- monohydrate. 10. The method of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the esterifying produces an alkali metal carbonate that neutralizes malonic acid in the culture. 1 1. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the malonic acid is contacted with an ammonium base selected from ammonia, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate and combinations thereof. 12. The method of claim 1 1, wherein the ammonium base is ammonium hydroxide. 13. The method of claim 1 1 or claim 12, wherein the salt is 14. The method of any one of claims 1 1-13, wherein the ammonium base neutralizes malonic acid in the culturing step. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the recombinant host cell produces at least 20%, at least 30% or at least 40% of malonic acid by weight of the carbon source. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the malonic acid titer is at least 80 g/1 at the conclusion of the fermentation. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the isolating comprises: separating a liquid fraction comprising the malonic acid salt from cells, cell debris and soluble impurities; crystallizing the malonic acid salt; and isolating the crystalline malonic acid salt. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the separating comprises centrifugation, microfiltration, ultrafiltration or combinations thereof. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the separation further comprises nanofiltration through a material selected from a ceramic membrane, a metal membrane, a polymer membrane, and a composite membrane. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the material comprises pores ranging in size from 0.0005 Î¼ÏÎ¹ to 0.005 Î¼ÏÎ¹, equating to a molecular weight cut-off of about 100 Daltons to about 2,000 Daltons. 21. A method of producing diethyl malonate, comprising: fermenting a recombinant host cell comprising a heterologous nucleic acid encoding a mutated malonyl-CoA hydrolase in fermentation broth and producing malonic acid; 

 contacting the malonic acid with an ammonium base to produce a malonic acid salt; isolating the malonic acid salt from the fermentation broth by: separating a liquid fraction comprising the malonic acid salt from cells, cell debris and soluble impurities; crystallizing the malonic acid salt; and isolating the crystalline malonic acid salt; esterifying the crystalline malonic acid salt with an alcohol using supercritical carbon dioxide (sCC ) or sulfuric acid as a catalyst to produce diethyl malonate; and distilling the diethyl malonate; wherein the ammonium base controls the pH of fermentation. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the host cell further comprises an integrating plasmid comprising an expression cassette with an AOXl promoter operably linked to the heterologous nucleic acid. 23. The method of claim 21 or claim 22, wherein the hydrolase comprises mutations selected from E95N, Q348A, F304R and combinations thereof. 24. The method of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutation E95N. 25. The method of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutations E95N and Q348A. 26. The method of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutations E95N and F304R. 27. The method of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutations E95N, Q348A and F304R. 28. The method of any one of claims 21-27, wherein the hydrolase is derived from P seudomonas fulva. 29. The method of any one of claims 21-28, wherein the fermentation is performed under aerobic conditions. 30. The method of any one of claims 21-29, wherein the ammonium base is selected from ammonia, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate and combinations thereof. 31. The method of any one of claims 21-30, wherein the ammonium base is ammonium hydroxide and the malonic acid salt is diammonium malonate. 32. The method of any one of claims 21-31, wherein the crystalline malonic acid salt is dried prior to esterification. 33. The method of any one of claims 21-32, further comprising hydrolyzing the diethyl malonate to produce malonic acid.
